title: Embedded Engineer
description: Design and develop software for embedded systems and hardware devices.
overview: Embedded systems engineering is a specialized field that combines electrical engineering and computer science. Engineers in this field design and develop the software and hardware that control countless devices we use every day, from microwaves and cars to medical equipment and industrial robots.
careerScope: Embedded engineers work in automotive, aerospace, consumer electronics, and medical device industries. Roles include Embedded Software Engineer, Firmware Engineer, and IoT Developer.

beginner.name: C Programming for Embedded
beginner.desc: Master memory management, pointers, and bitwise operations.

beginner.name: Computer Architecture
beginner.desc: Understand CPU, memory, and I/O registers.

intermediate.name: Microcontroller Interfaces
intermediate.desc: Learn UART, SPI, I2C, and GPIO on platforms like STM32 or AVR.

intermediate.name: Real-Time Operating Systems (RTOS)
intermediate.desc: Concurrency, task scheduling, and semaphores using FreeRTOS.

advanced.name: Device Drivers & Kernel
advanced.desc: Writing low-level drivers and understanding OS kernels.

projects.title: IoT Weather Station
projects.description: Build a weather station that reads sensors and sends data via MQTT.
